The traditional object detection models operate under the closed world assumption, while the proposed model explores unknown information to address the issue of encountering unknown objects during detection.
Open World Object Detection (OWOD) involves identifying both known and unknown object categories, as opposed to traditional object detection models.
OWOD aims to identify unknown objects that may not have appeared during the training phase and learn them incrementally once their annotations are provided.
OWOD pioneers the concept of Open World Object Detection, where detection models should be able to identify both known and unknown object categories.

edge: The proposed Annealing-based Label-Transfer OWOD model is the first OWOD work without manually selecting unknown proposals, achieving state-of-the-art performance.
Unlike previous models, the proposed Annealing-based Label-Transfer OWOD model not only reports the unknown recall performance but also presents the unknown mAP performance.
